mission.title: argo-events: Problem with setting SQS queue
mission.description: Problem with setting SQS queue . This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo-events troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo-events deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events -n argo-events --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review argo-events configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-events configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
kubectl get configmap -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-events
```
We are creating EventSource on EKS k8s cluster that should consume from SQS

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
```apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: EventSource
metadata:
  name: aws-sqs
spec:
  sqs:
    sqs-git:
      jsonBody:

steps.title: Apply the fix for Problem with setting SQS queue
steps.description:
Secrets edited by tools like `vim` always have an invisible `\n` in the end, and it is often neglected by the user, however this makes differences for some of the applications. We trim that `\n` to avoid issues.

Also upgraded `go-git` package to the latest.

Might fix: #1068 as well.
```yaml
we also have aws secrets as in the example:
```

steps.title: Upgrade argo-events to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade argo-events:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade argo-events argo-events/argo-events --namespace argo-events
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events
helm list -n argo-events
```

steps.title: Confirm Problem with setting SQS queue  is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events -n argo-events --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo-events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Secrets edited by tools like `vim` always have an invisible `\n` in the end, and it is often neglected by the user, however this makes differences for some of the applications. We trim that `\n` to avoid issues.

Also upgraded `go-git` package to the latest.

Might fix: #1068 as well.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-events installed or the issue environment reproducible.
